title: Smart Health AI
tagline: AI-powered preliminary symptom checker and health advisor
description:
A machine learning application that helps users get a preliminary understanding of their health symptoms. Built with Scikit-learn classification models and a clean Streamlit interface, the tool processes symptom inputs and returns probability-ranked condition suggestions along with health advice.

This was my first full end-to-end ML project — from data cleaning through model selection, evaluation, and deployment.
highlights: Trained on a dataset of 4000+ symptom-disease pairs
Implemented 3 classifiers (Decision Tree, Random Forest, Naïve Bayes) and compared accuracy
Clean Streamlit UI deployable with zero configuration
Added confidence thresholds to avoid overconfident predictions

title: Titanic Survival Predictor
description: My first Kaggle competition. Explored the Titanic dataset, performed feature engineering (family size, title extraction, age imputation), and trained an XGBoost model with cross-validation. Achieved ~0.80 accuracy placing in the top 15% of the leaderboard.
highlights: XGBoost with hyperparameter tuning via GridSearchCV
0.802 accuracy — top 15% on public Kaggle leaderboard

title: DSA Algorithm Visualizer
tagline: Interactive visual explanations of sorting and graph algorithms
description: A browser-based tool that animates common algorithms step by step. Built to help myself and classmates understand how Bubble Sort, Merge Sort, Binary Search, and BFS/DFS actually work under the hood. No external libraries — pure vanilla JS canvas animations.
highlights: Visualizes Bubble, Selection, Insertion, Merge, Quick Sort
BFS and DFS on a grid with step-by-step playback
Speed control and custom array input
